Ohio

Speech-Language Pathologist License in Ohio

Here's what you need to know.

Last updated 2026-02-25 · Sources verified

At a glance

Endorsement fee
$200
Processing time
4 to 6 weeks
Renewal fee
$120
Fingerprinting
Required
Board
Ohio Board of Speech-Language Pathology and Audiology
Path
ASLP-IC active
See the steps Primary board page

Last updated 2026-02-25.

Out-of-State Speech-Language Pathologists

Ohio is an active member of the ASLP-IC. If your home state also participates and your license is eligible, you can obtain a compact privilege to practice in Ohio.

If you do not qualify for the compact, you can apply for licensure using the traditional endorsement pathway detailed below.

Key Timeline Factors

Keep these requirements in mind, as they are the most likely to impact your processing timeline in Ohio.

  • Fingerprinting: Required. Out-of-state applicants can use IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects). Completing prints outside Ohio can add 2-4 weeks.
  • License verification: Ohio requires verification of your current out-of-state license before your file is complete.
  • Compact status: ASLP-IC is active in Ohio.
  • Temporary license: The board lists a temporary or provisional route while the full file is pending.

Ohio Endorsement Requirements

This is the step-by-step endorsement checklist for already-licensed speech-language pathologists applying to practice in Ohio.

  1. Step 1

    Submit Your Application

    Apply through the Ohio Board of Speech-Language Pathology and Audiology. The application fee is $200.

  2. Step 2

    Provide License Verification

    Ohio requires official verification of your active out-of-state license. This must be sent directly to the board from your current state board or its official online verification system before your file can be considered complete.

  3. Step 3

    Request Official graduate transcripts

    From a CAA-accredited speech-language pathology program.

  4. Step 4

    Submit Your Praxis exam score report

    Score of 162 or higher on test 5331.

  5. Step 5

    Verify Your Clinical fellowship verification

    Documentation of completed 9-month full-time or 18-month part-time supervised professional experience.

  6. Step 6

    Provide Background check and headshot

    Criminal background check and passport-style headshot photograph.

  7. Step 7

    Complete Fingerprinting and Background Screening

    Ohio requires a criminal background check for all applicants. Out-of-state applicants can use IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects). Completing prints outside Ohio can add 2-4 weeks to your processing time.

  8. Step 8

    Await Board Processing

    Plan for a processing timeline of about 4 to 6 weeks once your file is complete. If you need to start working immediately, the board offers a temporary or provisional licensing route that you can utilize while your full application is pending.

  9. Step 9

    Obtain a Conditional SLP License

    Apply for the Conditional SLP License ($210) to begin your clinical fellowship. Complete 9 months full-time or 18 months part-time of supervised clinical practice under an experienced SLP.

  10. Step 10

    Apply for Full SLP Licensure

    Submit the full SLP license application ($200) via the eLicense Ohio portal. Include official transcripts, Praxis scores, clinical fellowship verification, headshot photograph, and background check results.

Fees and Renewal

Total fee
$200
Processing time
4 to 6 weeks
Renewal fee
$120
Renewal cycle
Every 2 years (December 31, even years)
Application
$200
Background check
$0 (included in application)
Jurisprudence exam
$0
Total estimated cost
$200

Temporary License

Ohio offers a temporary or provisional license so you can start working while your full endorsement application is processing.

Fee
$210
Duration
See board
Processing time
See board

Required Documents

  • Official graduate transcripts: From a CAA-accredited speech-language pathology program.
  • Praxis exam score report: Score of 162 or higher on test 5331.
  • Clinical fellowship verification: Documentation of completed 9-month full-time or 18-month part-time supervised professional experience.
  • Background check and headshot: Criminal background check and passport-style headshot photograph.

Ohio Board of Speech-Language Pathology and Audiology
Department
Ohio Speech and Hearing Professionals Board (SHPB)
Phone
(614) 466-3145
Email

Board site
Primary board page
Compact source
ASLP-IC

Source: Primary board website

Renewal

Renewal fee is $120. Renewal cycle: Every 2 years (December 31, even years).

Ohio Speech-Language Pathologist Licensing FAQ

How much does an Ohio SLP license cost?

The initial SLP license application fee is $200, plus an additional $3.50 eLicense Ohio maintenance fee. A Conditional SLP License for the clinical fellowship costs $210. Biennial renewal is $120.

How many CE hours are required for Ohio SLP renewal?

Ohio requires 20 hours of continuing education every two years, with at least 2 hours in ethics and 10 hours directly related to clinical speech-language pathology practice. CE hours for the first renewal period are not required.

When does my Ohio SLP license expire?

Ohio SLP licenses for the 2025-2026 biennium are valid until December 31, 2026. Renewal notifications are sent via email starting October of the expiration year.

Does Ohio have reciprocity for speech-language pathologists?

Yes. Ohio participates in the ASLP-IC. If your home state and license qualify for compact privilege, you can typically practice in Ohio without a separate endorsement application.

How long does it take to transfer my speech-language pathologist license to Ohio?

Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 weeks. If you qualify for an active compact privilege, there may be no separate wait to start practicing.

Can I start working in Ohio while my endorsement application is processing?

A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.
